title: diploma altaica
intro: Diploma altaica, also known as the Amur cork tree, is a deciduous tree native to northeastern Asia and eastern Russia. The bark and roots of the Diploma altaica plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Diploma altaica include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, while also serving as a natural remedy for digestive issues and coughs. Herbal preparations of Diploma altaica include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to make capsules or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
